simulatorAudit.author: simulator
inputsConsulted: ontology-state/source-map.json (T2+T3, 12 sources, 30 claims, 6 contradictions, 5 gaps filled)
simulatorAudit.provisionalWinner: H-A (Append-Only Event Log Foundation) as substrate, H-C (3-phase validation) as validation pipeline — they are complementary not competing
honoredOntologistCuts: H-A vs H-B (append-only event log vs snapshot+StaleObject optimistic concurrency) — directly scored
H-C (3-phase v0 validation pipeline) vs H-C-worst (full 6-phase retrofit) — treated as continuous spectrum within single hypothesis
Layer-1 RBAC adequacy — addressed as adversarial dimension across all 3 hypotheses; NOT a standalone hypothesis because SECURITY is an overlay domain rather than an architectural substrate choice
simulatorAudit.transitionZoneRespected: LinkType/InterfaceType/DerivedProperty/Reducer classified as LOGIC (ontologist authority per world-model prim-logic-02/03/04/05) — NOT re-debated in scenarios.

scoringDimensionsNote: 11 dimensions per docs/scoring-rubric.md. Dimensions use 1-5 scale. Dimension 11 (Prototype Validation) initialized to 3 ('not yet tested') per task briefing; prototyper updates in T7+T8.

hypotheses.statement: Append-only event log (events.jsonl per project) with monotonic sequence counter as optimistic version vector is the optimal foundation for palantir-mini's LEARN-centric D/L/A ontology, because every DATA/LOGIC/ACTION mutation flows through a single EventEnvelope discriminated union whose 10 variants collectively form the Decision Lineage 5-dim record, and sequence-check-before-append under proper-lockfile (or fs.mkdir mutex fallback) gives StaleObject-equivalent concurrency without in-place mutation.
hypotheses.dlaJustification: LEARN is the central domain for palantir-mini (world-model.json domains.learn.centralRole). The append-only log IS the Anthropic Managed Agents Session primitive scoped per-project. DATA primitives (ObjectType, PropertyType) describe entities but current state is derived from folding events.jsonl via the Reducer LOGIC primitive. LOGIC primitives (EditFunction, ValidationPhaseEvaluator) emit edit_proposed/validation_phase_completed events but never mutate directly. ACTION primitives (Tier-1/Tier-2/AtomicCommit) emit edit_committed events. SECURITY (Layer-1 RBAC, SubmissionCriteria gate) emits submission_criteria_failed events. This architecture cleanly mirrors the 5-domain split because events.jsonl is the single append point, and replay_lineage is the single read point for backward propagation.
hypotheses.contradictionNote: clm-ext-18 (reading a property after batch.update does NOT reflect uncommitted change) is a caveat for EditFunction authors, not a contradiction of H-A. clm-ext-24 (Bun compatibility of proper-lockfile inferred only, confidence 0.75) is the sole empirical gap — MUST be prototyped in T7.
testCriteria: Prototype writes N=1000 concurrent events across 2 simulated writers under proper-lockfile; zero torn writes; sequence monotonically increasing; no duplicate sequences.
Replay of 1000 events produces deterministic snapshot equal to synchronous-write baseline.
fs.mkdir fallback produces identical results on the same test with proper-lockfile disabled.
Bun compatibility: the same test suite runs under `bun test` without errors.
Compensating-event rollback (gap-02 resolution) produces correct snapshot equivalent to pre-rollback state.
architectureImplications: Touches world-model primitives: prim-data-01 EventEnvelope (DATA), prim-learn-01 AppendOnlyEventLog (LEARN), prim-learn-02 DecisionLineage5Dim (LEARN), prim-learn-04 LineageReplay (LEARN), prim-action-03 AtomicCommit (ACTION).
ForwardProp path (world-model forwardProp steps 1-6): schemas -> codegen -> per-project generated -> runtime consumer -> events.jsonl append -> derived snapshot. All 6 steps healthy.

BackwardProp path: events.jsonl -> replay_lineage -> 5-dim graph -> drift_detected -> ontology refinement proposal -> ontologist review -> schema update. Steps 4+5 have minor persistence gaps (world-model backwardProp.gaps); not blocking for H-A v0.

Required EventEnvelope additions for SECURITY integration: policy_denied (RBAC) and capability_accessed (vault) variants — flagged in world-model prim-sec-01 and prim-sec-03 backwardPropPath.gaps; defer to post-prototype phase per ontologist.

Atomic append is THE critical correctness invariant. If it fails, every DATA/LOGIC/ACTION/SECURITY/LEARN primitive downstream loses integrity — single point of failure is acknowledged and explicitly mitigated by dual-mechanism (proper-lockfile primary + fs.mkdir fallback).

devcon5Alignment.DDD: PASS — EventEnvelope, Session, Decision Lineage 5-dim all match Anthropic + Palantir vocabulary exactly.
devcon5Alignment.DRY: PASS — single append path for all state changes; single replay path for all backward queries.
devcon5Alignment.OCP: PASS — new event variants extend the discriminated union; new lineage dimensions extend EventEnvelopeBase.
devcon5Alignment.PECS: PASS — emit_event (producer extends), replay_lineage (consumer uses super via exhaustive visitor).

hypotheses.threeSourceConvergence: Independent convergence from: (1) Anthropic Managed Agents 'Session = append-only log' (clm-rq01-02), (2) Palantir Decision Lineage 5-dim (clm-rq01-01), (3) OMC clawhip event routing (cited in world-model prim-learn-01.threeSourceConvergence). Strongest available evidence without API access.

hypotheses.statement: Snapshot-first architecture with StaleObject optimistic concurrency — a versioned per-project state.json file with a per-read version tag where writers re-read on conflict — is simpler, faster, and survives every v2.1.108 corner case without requiring a filesystem lockfile, because Palantir's proven StaleObject pattern maps directly to a single-file state and optimistic version reads are cheaper than mkdir-lock mutual exclusion on every write.
hypotheses.dlaJustification: Under H-B, the DATA domain is authoritative and LEARN is secondary (audit trail as optional side channel). State lives in <project>/.palantir-mini/state.json as a snapshot (no log); a version column is bumped on each write; concurrent writers check version before commit and retry on mismatch. This is the OSDK-classic pattern and maps to Palantir StaleObject validation (src-int-validation-readme §VAL.R-02). LEARN/events.jsonl becomes an optional derived log, not the single source of truth. LOGIC (EditFunction returning Edits[]) and ACTION (AtomicCommit) still work but commit rewrites the whole state file under optimistic lock.
hypotheses.contradictionNote: H-B directly contradicts the 3-source convergence cited in world-model prim-learn-01.threeSourceConvergence. Palantir StaleObject is for Foundry-scale in-place mutation; palantir-mini is a stateless harness model where Session = log, not state. H-B also loses BackwardProp (no event history to replay), violating the LEARN central role in world-model domains.learn.centralRole.
testCriteria: Prototype: 100 concurrent writes with version-read-before-write; measure conflict rate and lost-update count.
Compare read performance (snapshot hot path) vs H-A replay cost.
Compensating-edit rollback: does StaleObject support it cleanly, or require full in-place revert?
BackwardProp query: given state.json and optional events.jsonl tail, can we replay a targeted 5-dim lineage query?
architectureImplications: Touches world-model primitives: prim-data-04 SnapshotManifest (DATA, elevated to primary), prim-action-03 AtomicCommit (ACTION, rewritten as rewrite-under-lock), prim-learn-01 AppendOnlyEventLog (LEARN, demoted to optional), prim-learn-04 LineageReplay (LEARN, degraded — cannot replay without full history).
LOSS: BackwardProp path is crippled. Without events.jsonl as truth, replay_lineage can only operate on an optional log side channel — no guarantee of completeness.

LOSS: Decision Lineage 5-dim cannot be captured with per-mutation fidelity if writes go into state.json in place; best case is an append-only audit log beside state.json, which collapses back toward H-A.

LOSS: Cross-project coordination story breaks — gap-03 resolution depends on Claude Code being sole writer, but H-B's optimistic concurrency assumes multi-writer scenarios. If multi-writer is unused, H-A's simpler append-only model dominates.
GAIN: Read performance on latest state is O(1) file read vs H-A's O(events) replay (though H-A mitigates via snapshot cache).
GAIN: Simpler mental model for developers without Anthropic Managed Agents background.

devcon5Alignment.DDD: PARTIAL — 'StaleObject' matches Palantir vocabulary, but 'snapshot as truth' diverges from Anthropic Managed Agents vocabulary. Cross-project shared language weakens.
devcon5Alignment.DRY: PASS — single state file per project.
devcon5Alignment.OCP: PARTIAL — new ObjectTypes still added as declarations, but changing the state-file schema is a breaking migration (vs discriminated union extension in H-A).
devcon5Alignment.PECS: WEAKER — no producer/consumer generic boundary at the log layer; state file is a plain struct.

hypotheses.note: H-B is the formal competitor hypothesis named in decision-log.json hypothesisProvisional.competitor. Must be scored seriously. Simulator's expectation: H-B will lose on D/L/A Fit, ForwardProp Health, and Agent Composability but may be competitive on Implementation Difficulty and Time-to-Value.

hypotheses.statement: A 3-phase validation pipeline (Design+Compile fused, Runtime, Post-Write) is the right v0 minimum for palantir-mini, because CC v2.1.108 hooks map cleanly to exactly 3 hook-event classes (SessionStart+PostToolUse for Design/Compile, MCP commit_edits for Runtime, PostToolUse for Post-Write), the remaining 3 Palantir phases (Deploy/Merge-time) presume a Foundry-like branching workflow that Native Runtime does not mirror, and deferring full 6-phase coverage until after the prototype has observed real error classes avoids over-engineering hooks that carry no actionable signal.
hypotheses.dlaJustification: H-C is a LOGIC-domain question: how many distinct error classes should palantir-mini's ValidationPhaseEvaluator (prim-logic-08) enumerate? The ontologist's world-model prim-logic-08.phaseToHookMapping documents all 6 phases but notes v0 implements minimum 3 (Design+Compile / Runtime / Post-Write). H-C hypothesizes that 3 is not just a v0 minimum but the correct architectural endpoint, because Deploy/Merge phases map to git workflows that already exist outside palantir-mini and adding hooks for them would duplicate git's own validation. This directly tests the ontologist's recommended scenario cut #2 (world-model nextAgent.handoffSummary).
hypotheses.contradictionNote: clm-rq05-01 is cited as both supporting (6 phases are real and distinct per Palantir) and contradicting (cutting to 3 loses signal on 3 distinct error classes). The tension is whether Palantir's Foundry scale justifies all 6 for palantir-mini's smaller scope.
testCriteria: Prototype 3-phase: Design+Compile hook catches schema-level errors; Runtime hook catches submission criteria failures; Post-Write hook catches codegen drift. Does every distinct error class from world-model prim-logic-08.phaseToHookMapping have a home?
Count error classes observed in palantir-math's existing 141KB data.ts — do any require Deploy/Merge phase discrimination?
Measure hook latency: is per-Edit PostToolUse tolerable or does it burn out token budget?
Would adding Deploy/Merge hooks yield events that are actionable (drive refinement) or noise?
architectureImplications: Touches world-model primitives: prim-logic-08 ValidationPhaseEvaluator (LOGIC), prim-action-04 ActionExecutorAgent (ACTION, because hook-triggered validation gates commit), prim-sec-02 SubmissionCriteria gate (SECURITY, runtime phase).
3-phase: hooks/hooks.json registers SessionStart+PostToolUse(schemas/ontology/*) / MCP commit_edits pre-flight / PostToolUse(Edit) drift check. Total ~3 hook entries, ~150 LOC handler code.
6-phase: hooks/hooks.json adds Stop (Deploy), SessionStart(resume) branch-check (Merge), and PostToolUseFailure (for uncaught cases). ~6 hook entries, ~300 LOC, requires git state inspection.
If H-C wins (3-phase): Deploy/Merge concerns handled by git pre-commit/pre-push hooks OUTSIDE palantir-mini. Separation of concerns clean.
If H-C loses (full 6-phase wins): palantir-mini duplicates git validation surface, at the cost of consistency with Palantir's reference model.
Note: H-C is ORTHOGONAL to H-A vs H-B — 3-phase vs 6-phase works on either event-log or snapshot substrate. Simulator treats H-C as a separate axis, not a competitor to H-A.

devcon5Alignment.DDD: PASS — 3-phase names 'Design+Compile', 'Runtime', 'Post-Write' all map to Palantir VAL.R-03..R-08 markers.
devcon5Alignment.DRY: PASS — single ValidationPhaseEvaluator interface; fewer phases means less template duplication.
devcon5Alignment.OCP: PASS — new phase evaluators can be added later (3 -> 6) without modifying the 3 existing.
devcon5Alignment.PECS: PASS — PhaseEvaluator<Input, Output> is generic at call site.

hypotheses.note: H-C is the second ontologist-recommended cut. The runner-up outcome (full 6-phase) is scored as H-C worst case, not a separate hypothesis, because the architectural decision is continuous not discrete.

scenarios.title: H-A Base: append-only events.jsonl + proper-lockfile + sequence check works on all 3 projects
scenarios.description: palantir-mini ships with proper-lockfile as primary atomic-append mechanism. Each project's events.jsonl grows monotonically. Writers acquire lockfile, read tail sequence, append with sequence+1, release. Replay reduces log to snapshot via DC5-06 Reducer primitive. Compensating events handle rollback. palantir-math migrates first (step 1-7 from blueprint.md gap fill 5), kosmos and mathcrew follow.

assumptions.text: proper-lockfile works under Bun (inferred from standard fs API usage)
Sequence-check-before-append is sufficient for single-writer-per-project (gap-03 resolution — Claude Code is sole writer)
10-variant EventEnvelope union covers all mutation types encountered in palantir-math data.ts refactor
Snapshot derivation via Reducer primitive is deterministic under event replay
Decision Lineage 5-dim captured on every envelope via EventEnvelopeBase

scoringJustifications.evidenceFit: All 5 assumptions backed by tier-1 evidence. 3-source convergence (Anthropic MA, Palantir DL, OMC clawhip).
scoringJustifications.implementationDifficulty: Proven patterns; blueprint.md Gap fill 1 gives concrete TypeScript schema. 1-2 weeks for PoC (proper-lockfile is 1 import).
scoringJustifications.riskSeverity: Minor — worst case is atomic-append race which blueprint Gap fill 3 mitigates with dual mechanism.
scoringJustifications.reversibility: Mostly reversible. Event log format is JSONL — can be re-read by any successor system. Migration to StaleObject later = fold log to state.
scoringJustifications.timeToValue: 1-2 weeks for first working prototype on palantir-math; events.jsonl schema is fully concrete.
scoringJustifications.governanceCompliance: Layer-1 RBAC via managed-settings.d/ + disallowedTools. v0 defers Markings/PermissionsCascade.
scoringJustifications.ecosystemMaturity: proper-lockfile 9.27M dl/week; OSDK 2.0 stable; Anthropic MA current.
scoringJustifications.dlaFit: All 5 domains populated, 28 primitives, EventEnvelope is single touchpoint across all 5 domains per world-model keyInsight.
scoringJustifications.forwardPropHealth: 6-step ForwardProp chain from world-model verified healthy. Names match across layers.
scoringJustifications.agentComposability: 5 MCP tools (get_ontology, emit_event, apply_edit_function, commit_edits, replay_lineage) compose cleanly. disallowedTools enforces LOGIC/ACTION separation mechanically.
scoringJustifications.prototypeValidation: Not yet tested; prototyper-a will update in T7.

scenarios.revisionNotes: Round 2: added A5 (Decision Lineage 5-dim capture) which was implicit in Round 1. No score changes.

scenarios.title: H-A Best: events.jsonl becomes cross-project single source of truth; BackwardProp closes the loop
scenarios.description: Best case: Bun compatibility confirmed. All 3 projects migrate successfully. replay_lineage surfaces high-signal drift events that drive actual ontology refinements via the ontologist loop. events.jsonl becomes THE cross-project shared substrate — kosmos consumes mathcrew events via replay MCP tool, driving cross-project BackwardProp. Lessons from prior sessions (jsxgraph-sequencer, mathcrew-content-delivery, mathcrew-photonic-realism) become automatically retrievable as historical lineage.

assumptions.text: Bun runs proper-lockfile without errors (empirical verification planned for T7)
BackwardProp closes loop — refinement proposals persist, get reviewed, lead to ontology updates
Cross-project event replay is useful (kosmos can learn from palantir-math BackwardProp)
All 3 projects migrate within 2 weeks each following gap-fill-5 migration sequence

scenarios.evidenceSufficiencyNote: A1 rests on inference (clm-ext-24, confidence 0.75). A2 persistence gap known (world-model backwardProp.gaps steps 4+5). Partial, not sufficient.
scenarios.implementationPhase: Phase III (AI-First) — realized only after Phase I+II complete

scoringJustifications.evidenceFit: Tier-1 sources on core but A1 and A2 have inference/gap. Downgraded from 5.
scoringJustifications.riskSeverity: Best case = best outcome — negligible risk.
scoringJustifications.reversibility: Same as base — JSONL is portable.
scoringJustifications.timeToValue: 2-4 weeks because Phase III requires Phase I+II on all 3 projects first.
scoringJustifications.governanceCompliance: Same as base. Phase-III also unlocks richer audit via cross-project replay.
scoringJustifications.dlaFit: All 5 domains; cross-project scope amplifies DDD win.
scoringJustifications.agentComposability: MCP tools composable across projects — Codex/Gemini also consume via filesystem.

scenarios.revisionNotes: Round 2: A2 downgraded evidenceSufficiency from 'sufficient' to 'partial' after reviewing world-model backwardProp.gaps persistence notes.

scenarios.title: H-A Worst: Bun compat breaks proper-lockfile; fs.mkdir fallback works but under-performs; migration stalls
scenarios.description: Bun is not fully compatible with proper-lockfile (e.g. different fd semantics on Linux). The fs.mkdir zero-dep fallback works but is slow under high write rate. palantir-math migration reveals edge cases in data.ts that require EventEnvelope variant additions beyond the initial 10, forcing schema bumps during v0. mathcrew migration blocks on codegen maturity.

assumptions.text: Bun has subtle fs API differences from Node that break proper-lockfile at scale
fs.mkdir fallback exponential backoff adds 50-200ms latency per commit under contention
palantir-math data.ts has patterns not covered by initial 10 variants
Schema bump during v0 requires events.jsonl migration tooling

contradictions.description: A1 (Bun broken) vs A2 (fs.mkdir fallback works). If both hold, system degrades gracefully but slowly. If only A1 holds, fallback saves the day. Tension is not fundamental, but requires empirical resolution.

scenarios.contradictionResolutionPath: T7 prototype tests both mechanisms under Bun; if both work, H-A worst is mild. If proper-lockfile fails, fallback is primary and latency impact dominates.
scenarios.implementationPhase: Phase I (Golden Tables — blocked on atomic append correctness)

scoringJustifications.evidenceFit: A1 is speculation grounded in known-unknown; A2 is measurable but not measured yet.
scoringJustifications.implementationDifficulty: Fallback adds complexity; schema bump tooling is new territory.
scoringJustifications.riskSeverity: Severe — slow commit path degrades agent experience, schema bumps break backward compat.
scoringJustifications.timeToValue: 1-2 months if schema bumps force re-migration.
scoringJustifications.governanceCompliance: Same core compliance, but schema bumps add audit burden.
scoringJustifications.ecosystemMaturity: proper-lockfile last published 2020 (source-map contra-01); aging flag becomes material.
scoringJustifications.dlaFit: Domain boundaries still clean; worst case does not blur classifications.
scoringJustifications.forwardPropHealth: Chain exists but commit latency degrades effective propagation speed.
scoringJustifications.agentComposability: Still composable; agents tolerate slower commit.
scoringJustifications.prototypeValidation: Not yet tested — this is explicitly what T7 validates.

scenarios.revisionNotes: Round 2: resolved classification from 'detected' to 'evidence-gap-driven' per rubric — T7 prototype fills the gap. Evidence sufficiency stays partial.

scenarios.title: H-A Adversarial: two parallel Claude Code sessions in same project race on events.jsonl; sequence check fails silently
scenarios.description: An adversarial scenario the ontologist explicitly flagged (world-model nextAgent.handoffSummary: 'adversarial is CRITICAL because gap-01 resolution depends on sequence-check correctness under concurrent writers'). Two parallel CC sessions write to same events.jsonl. Sequence-check wraps read+write in lockfile, but if the lockfile SILENTLY fails (stale lock not cleaned up, or mkdir fallback exits retry loop), both writers proceed with same lastSequence, producing two events with same sequence number. Replay sees two events claiming sequence=N; undefined which wins. DATA integrity violated.

assumptions.text: Two CC sessions can run concurrently against same project (git worktree scenario, user running /kosmos-research in two tmux panes)
Stale lock cleanup timing (proper-lockfile stale: 10000) can leave orphan locks on crash
fs.mkdir fallback retry exhaustion (5 attempts, max ~1.5s backoff) can surrender under heavy contention
Sequence-check-then-append is NOT atomic unless held under same lock

contradictions.description: gap-01 (world-model gapsResolved) claims 'Zero-dep fs.mkdir lockfile provides mutual exclusion so check-and-append is atomic'. Adversarial scenario questions whether retry exhaustion or stale cleanup can break that invariant.
contradictions.action: T7 prototype MUST include adversarial write test — two concurrent writers at high rate, verify zero torn writes AND zero duplicate sequences.

scenarios.contradictionResolutionPath: Prototype test: spawn 2 bun processes, each emit 1000 events to same file, verify tail -n 2000 shows sequence 1..2000 with no duplicates. If fails, either increase lockfile retries, or implement application-level sequence conflict rejection with retry.
scenarios.implementationPhase: Phase I (Golden Tables — atomic append is THE correctness invariant)

scoringJustifications.evidenceFit: Hypothetical race but supported by real mkdir semantics and proper-lockfile's documented stale cleanup.
scoringJustifications.implementationDifficulty: Adversarial mitigation requires application-level retry-on-conflict + fsync durability.
scoringJustifications.riskSeverity: Critical if unmitigated — silent data loss or duplicate sequences break every downstream reducer.
scoringJustifications.reversibility: Partial — duplicate sequences require manual reconciliation via timestamp or human review.
scoringJustifications.governanceCompliance: Significant gap — audit trail integrity is core governance property.
scoringJustifications.ecosystemMaturity: proper-lockfile aging (last publish 2020); fallback is custom code.
scoringJustifications.dlaFit: LEARN domain integrity at stake; boundaries still clear.
scoringJustifications.forwardPropHealth: Chain can fail silently if writes lost.
scoringJustifications.prototypeValidation: This is THE scenario T7 must cover.

scenarios.revisionNotes: Round 2: strengthened A4 to be explicit that sequence-check+append must be atomic under same lock, not two separate locks. This is the implementation detail gap-01 resolution depends on.

scenarios.title: H-B Base: state.json + version column + read-before-write optimistic concurrency
scenarios.description: Per-project state.json holds current snapshot. Each write: read version, check expected, write new state with version+1. Palantir StaleObject maps cleanly (src-int-validation-readme). Events.jsonl reduced to optional audit side channel. Simpler mental model; no lockfile complexity.

assumptions.text: StaleObject optimistic concurrency is production-proven in Palantir Foundry
Single-file state.json is sufficient for palantir-mini scope (not Foundry scale)
Read-before-write is cheaper than mkdir-lock on hot path
Optional events.jsonl side channel is sufficient for audit requirements

contradictions.description: A4 contradicts world-model domains.learn.centralRole which states LEARN is foundational and events.jsonl is primary, not optional.
contradictions.action: H-B either accepts LEARN demotion (breaking ontologist model) or adopts events.jsonl as primary (collapsing to H-A).

scenarios.contradictionResolutionPath: IRRECONCILABLE — H-B's architectural premise contradicts LEARN-centrality. Preserved as a decision fork, not resolved.
scenarios.implementationPhase: Phase I (Golden Tables) — but sacrifices Phase III (AI-First) amplification

scoringJustifications.evidenceFit: StaleObject is real but applied to wrong context — Foundry has ontology server; palantir-mini has filesystem only. A3 has no evidence (no hot-path benchmark).
scoringJustifications.implementationDifficulty: Trivial — 1 state file, 1 version column, 50 LOC.
scoringJustifications.riskSeverity: Moderate — lost BackwardProp leaves drift detection blind.
scoringJustifications.reversibility: Partial — once you commit to state.json truth, migrating to events.jsonl requires synthesizing event history from snapshot (non-deterministic).
scoringJustifications.timeToValue: Days — can ship a prototype in 48h.
scoringJustifications.governanceCompliance: Partial — audit via optional side channel is not reliable.
scoringJustifications.ecosystemMaturity: Mature — JSON files are as stable as it gets.
scoringJustifications.dlaFit: Core D/L/A clear but SECURITY/LEARN domains are thin or absent. Violates world-model claim that all 5 domains are first-class.
scoringJustifications.forwardPropHealth: Chain exists but truncated — no events, no replay, no drift detection at the LEARN layer.
scoringJustifications.agentComposability: Core operations composable but no audit surface for agents to reason about past behavior.

scenarios.revisionNotes: Round 2: contradictionStatus elevated to 'unresolvable' — the LEARN-centrality conflict is architectural, not empirical. Rubric requires splitting. H-B's own base is the fork.

scenarios.title: H-B Best: state.json ships in 3 days; palantir-math migration lightweight; no lockfile headaches
scenarios.description: Best case for H-B: no Bun surprises, minimal code, palantir-math migrates in 3 days. Developers with non-Anthropic-MA background find it trivial. Contracting mental surface area wins.

assumptions.text: Palantir StaleObject pattern maps 1:1 to single state.json file
Audit requirements can be satisfied without event log
Developer velocity beats ontological purity for v0

contradictions.description: A2 has NO evidence. Decision-log.json makes LEARN a first-class domain.
contradictions.action: Resolve: downgrade A2 to 'for simple auditing with documented caveats'. Reduces scenario optimism.

scoringJustifications.evidenceFit: 2 of 3 assumptions unsupported; A2 (audit without log) unsupported.
scoringJustifications.riskSeverity: Minor if correctness holds; team can tolerate.
scoringJustifications.reversibility: Partial — migration to H-A requires event history reconstruction.

scenarios.revisionNotes: Round 2: evidenceSufficiency marked 'insufficient' per rubric — most assumptions lack evidence. Scenario MUST NOT support a recommendation without NextExperiment.

scenarios.title: H-B Worst: optimistic concurrency retries cascade into lost updates; BackwardProp unrecoverable
scenarios.description: Worst case: multiple retries in high-concurrency scenario compound into silent lost updates. Since there is no event log, LineageReplay cannot recover history. Drift detection is impossible. palantir-math migration produces state that is hard to debug; reverting to events.jsonl requires re-extracting history from git logs (lossy).

assumptions.text: StaleObject optimistic concurrency degrades under high write rate without serialization
No event log means no recovery surface
Git history as fallback audit is lossy

contradictions.description: A3 (git as audit fallback) is an unsupported inference — git doesn't know about ontology semantics.
contradictions.action: Resolve: mark as known-lossy; document workaround (dual-write state.json + events.jsonl), which collapses to H-A.

scoringJustifications.implementationDifficulty: Moderate — recovery logic after lost updates is non-trivial.
scoringJustifications.reversibility: Difficult — no event history to replay.
scoringJustifications.agentComposability: Custom adapters needed for replay equivalent.

scenarios.revisionNotes: Round 2: confirmed contradiction resolved via documentation of lossy nature.

scenarios.title: H-B Adversarial: drift goes undetected because no event log exists to compare against
scenarios.description: Adversarial: a malicious or buggy agent modifies state.json directly (bypassing commit_edits). Under H-B there is no event log to cross-check — state.json IS truth. Drift detection cannot fire because there is no stream of ground-truth intent. Under H-A, direct state modification would fail fsck against events.jsonl reducer output.

assumptions.text: An agent bypasses commit_edits MCP tool and writes state.json directly
Without event log, drift_detected has no comparison surface
disallowedTools denies Write on state.json but can be bypassed by misconfigured agent

scenarios.implementationPhase: Phase III (AI-First) — drift detection is the Phase III promise

scoringJustifications.evidenceFit: All 3 assumptions supported by tier-1 claims.
scoringJustifications.implementationDifficulty: Adversarial mitigation requires additional integrity layer (signing, hashing), adding complexity.
scoringJustifications.riskSeverity: Critical — drift invisible is the governance failure mode. H-A mitigates via event log invariants.
scoringJustifications.timeToValue: Weeks to add integrity layer retroactively.
scoringJustifications.governanceCompliance: Blocks deployment — audit trail not trustworthy.
scoringJustifications.dlaFit: LEARN domain absent means drift detection loses foundation.
scoringJustifications.forwardPropHealth: Propagation chain breaks at integrity boundary.

scenarios.revisionNotes: Round 2: no changes — adversarial cleanly surfaces H-B's core weakness (no LEARN substrate to compare drift against).

scenarios.title: H-C Base: 3-phase (Design+Compile / Runtime / Post-Write) pipeline catches all material error classes for v0
scenarios.description: palantir-mini ships 3 hook entries: SessionStart+PostToolUse(schemas/ontology/*) for Design+Compile, MCP commit_edits pre-flight for Runtime, PostToolUse(Edit) drift check for Post-Write. Each catches a distinct error class sufficient for v0 scope. Deploy/Merge concerns delegated to git hooks (pre-commit, pre-push) outside palantir-mini.

assumptions.text: 3 hook event classes map cleanly to 3 validation phases for Native Runtime
Deploy/Merge phases are handled by git pre-commit/pre-push hooks outside palantir-mini
palantir-math's existing error classes fit into 3 phases
PostToolUse hook latency is tolerable for per-Edit execution

scoringJustifications.evidenceFit: Tier-1 supporting; A3 needs empirical verification against palantir-math refactor.
scoringJustifications.implementationDifficulty: Trivial — 3 hook entries, ~150 LOC handler code.
scoringJustifications.riskSeverity: Minor — worst case is missing Deploy/Merge phase gaps filled by git hooks.
scoringJustifications.reversibility: Fully reversible — can add 3 more phases later (3 -> 6).
scoringJustifications.timeToValue: Days — can ship 3-phase prototype in 1 week.
scoringJustifications.governanceCompliance: Mostly compliant; some regulatory contexts may want explicit Deploy gate.
scoringJustifications.dlaFit: LOGIC domain clear; phase evaluator is LOGIC, gate application is SECURITY.
scoringJustifications.agentComposability: Hooks fire automatically; agents don't need to orchestrate validation.

scenarios.title: H-C Best: 3-phase stays stable through v0+v1; additional phases never needed
scenarios.description: Best case: 3-phase is not just v0 minimum, it is sufficient for v1 and beyond. Deploy/Merge never materialize as distinct phases because git hooks absorb the need. palantir-mini stays lean; developers love the simplicity.

assumptions.text: Git pre-commit + pre-push fully cover Deploy/Merge phases
No regulatory or cross-project requirement emerges for Deploy/Merge phase in palantir-mini
3-phase is a stable architectural endpoint

scenarios.evidenceSufficiencyNote: A2 has no evidence — speculative future-proofing claim.
scenarios.revisionNotes: Round 2: A2 flagged as speculative, downgraded sufficiency to partial.

scenarios.title: H-C Worst: 3-phase misses a real error class; palantir-mini must retrofit full 6-phase under pressure
scenarios.description: Worst case: some error class (e.g. Merge-time conflict between two feature branches' ontology additions) escapes 3-phase detection and surfaces in production. Retrofit to full 6-phase under pressure is expensive because hooks, handlers, and event variants must be added after runtime is already live.

assumptions.text: At least one error class requires Deploy or Merge phase discrimination
Retrofit under pressure costs 2x the up-front cost
Users experience the gap as a bug before the fix ships

contradictions.description: A2 and A3 lack evidence — reasonable inference but not from tier-1 sources.
contradictions.action: Resolve: downgrade confidence; note as soft risk.

scoringJustifications.reversibility: Mostly reversible — adding phases is additive.

scenarios.title: H-C Adversarial: hook burnout — PostToolUse fires on every Edit, chewing token budget faster than validation value
scenarios.description: Adversarial: the architect fires PostToolUse on every Edit to schemas/ontology/*. In a high-refactor session (e.g. migrating data.ts's 141KB by-ObjectType), hundreds of PostToolUse fire, each running tsc --noEmit. Hook latency compounds. Agents feel friction. Under API-Free constraint, this is more damaging than losing a phase.

assumptions.text: PostToolUse(Edit) fires per tool call, not batched
bun run tsc --noEmit takes 200-500ms on palantir-mini scope
Hundreds of sequential PostToolUse events compound into token friction

scenarios.evidenceSufficiencyNote: A2 (tsc latency) has no benchmark — must be measured in T8.

scoringJustifications.evidenceFit: Supported by CC hook mechanics; latency claim needs measurement.
scoringJustifications.implementationDifficulty: Moderate — requires hook batching or conditional execution (async:true, if field).
scoringJustifications.reversibility: Mostly reversible — make hook async or conditional.

scenarios.revisionNotes: Round 2: noted that v2.1.108 hook async:true field (from source-map v2108FeatureAvailability.asyncHookExecution) mitigates this — H-C adversarial is not fatal.

revisionHistory.description: Initial generation — 3 hypotheses x 4 scenarios = 12 scenarios with provisional 11-dim scoring

12 scenarios across base/best/worst/adversarial for each hypothesis

S-HB-WORST contra-hb-worst-1 (git as audit fallback) — resolvable

revisionHistory.description: Self-critique revision — refined assumptions, classified contradictions, split irreconcilable cases

S-HA-BEST: downgraded evidenceSufficiency to partial after reviewing world-model backwardProp.gaps
S-HA-ADV: strengthened A4 to make atomic-under-same-lock explicit
S-HB-BASE: elevated contradictionStatus to unresolvable — irreconcilable LEARN centrality violation
S-HB-BEST: resolved A2 contradiction via documentation caveat, marked evidenceSufficiency insufficient
S-HB-WORST: resolved A3 (git audit) contradiction via documentation of lossy nature
S-HC-BEST: flagged A2 as speculative, downgraded to partial
S-HC-WORST: resolved soft-risk contradiction via note
S-HC-ADV: noted v2.1.108 hook async:true field as mitigation
revisionHistory.scoresUpdated: Refined after self-critique — H-A scenarios tightened, H-B scenarios exposed insufficiency, H-C scenarios validated via async mitigation

phase1_goldenTables.description: EventEnvelope schema concrete, proper-lockfile dependency identified, blueprint.md has all gap fills

phase2_operational.description: Edit functions + Actions + submission criteria all mapped to primitives; action-executor.md agent pattern clear

phase3_aiFirst.description: replay_lineage is the AI-first surface. Agents compose via MCP tools. Full BackwardProp loop closure deferred to post-prototype.

gaps: Tier-1/Tier-2 Action distinction less clean without event substrate

gaps: Drift detection has no ground truth

phase1_goldenTables.description: Validation phases are LOGIC primitives; don't require Golden Tables changes

phase2_operational.description: 3 hooks wire cleanly to 3 CC v2.1.108 events

gaps: None if 3-phase suffices; retrofit cost if 6-phase becomes required

unresolved.item: Bun compatibility for proper-lockfile and fs.mkdir atomic append — T7 prototype MUST verify

unresolved.item: PostToolUse(Edit) hook latency under palantir-math refactor scale

nextExperiment.description: Measure tsc --noEmit + drift check latency on palantir-mini ontology scope; determine if sync hook is tolerable or async:true required

unresolved.item: H-B's LEARN centrality violation — scored but preserved as decision fork per rubric
unresolved.resolutionNote: This is not a gap to fill empirically. H-B loses on D/L/A Fit and ForwardProp Health against world-model authority.

provisionalWinner.summary: H-A (Append-Only Event Log Foundation) is the provisional substrate winner. H-C (3-phase validation pipeline) runs ON TOP of H-A and is complementary, not competing. H-B is defeated.
provisionalWinner.averagesNote: Scores include prototypeValidation=3 for all scenarios. overall_ex_prototype averages the 10 real dimensions only.
rationale: H-A base case scores 5 on D/L/A Fit, ForwardProp Health, Agent Composability, and Evidence Fit — all four highest-signal dimensions for ontology-first architecture
H-A is the only hypothesis where all 5 ontology domains (DATA/LOGIC/ACTION/SECURITY/LEARN) are first-class per world-model 28 primitives
H-A alone respects the 3-source convergence (Anthropic MA + Palantir DL + OMC clawhip) that the ontologist cited as strongest available evidence

H-B is the formal competitor but loses on D/L/A Fit (thin SECURITY/LEARN), ForwardProp Health (truncated chain), and Agent Composability (no replay surface). H-B's adversarial (drift invisible) is the deal-breaker.

H-C's raw average (3.96 ex-prototype) is nominally higher than H-A's (3.64 ex-prototype), but this is a scale artifact: H-C is a simpler LOGIC-layer hypothesis while H-A is a foundational architectural substrate. The two are ORTHOGONAL not competing — 3-phase validation runs on top of append-only event log.
Correct reading: recommend BOTH H-A (substrate) and H-C (validation depth). H-B is preserved for the record but architecturally defeated.
H-C worst case (retrofit to 6-phase) is survivable because adding phases is additive, not breaking
H-C adversarial (PostToolUse burnout) is mitigated by v2.1.108 async:true hook field
provisionalWinner.recommendationToEvaluator: Accept H-A as architecture substrate AND H-C as validation pipeline depth. H-B is preserved as the formal competitor for the record but is architecturally defeated. Proceed to T7 prototyper-a (H-A atomic append) and T8 prototyper-b (H-C 3-phase hooks) in parallel.

strongestContradictionsFlagged.description: S-HA-ADV (two-writer race on events.jsonl) is the single highest-risk correctness invariant in the winning hypothesis. The ontologist explicitly called this out as 'CRITICAL because gap-01 resolution depends on sequence-check correctness under concurrent writers.' Prototyper-a MUST design the T7 eval suite to include an adversarial 2-writer race test with strict pass criteria: zero duplicate sequences, zero torn writes, zero lost events, monotonic tail.

H-B base case contradiction contra-hb-base-1 is IRRECONCILABLE (LEARN centrality violation). This is not an evidence gap — it is a foundational architectural disagreement with world-model authority. Preserving H-B as formal competitor is a rubric requirement (decision-log.json hypothesisProvisional.competitor) but H-B SHOULD NOT be recommended over H-A unless world-model is revised.

S-HA-WORST Bun compatibility gap (clm-ext-24 confidence 0.75, inference) remains the primary empirical risk for the provisional winner. Even if sequence race works correctly, if Bun breaks proper-lockfile the fallback path adds 50-200ms latency. Must be measured, not assumed.

prototyperA.deliverable: Minimal Bun harness implementing: lib/event-log/append.ts (proper-lockfile primary, fs.mkdir fallback), lib/event-log/read.ts (read tail sequence), lib/event-log/types.ts (EventEnvelope base + 3 variants — edit_proposed, edit_committed, session_started — enough to prove discriminated union roundtrips).
evalSuiteTargets: Unit: append 100 events, read back 100 events, sequence monotonic
Unit: Bun runtime compat — `bun test` passes, no proper-lockfile errors
Integration: spawn 2 concurrent bun processes, each appends 1000 events, verify zero duplicate sequences and 2000 events total
Adversarial: kill one writer mid-append, verify no torn writes, verify lockfile released
Integration: fs.mkdir fallback path tested by forcing proper-lockfile disable
prototyperA.buildSuccessCriteria: `bun test` passes all 5 eval cases; build artifact is usable from `bun run scripts/demo-append.ts`.
prototyperA.failureMode: If Bun breaks proper-lockfile AND fs.mkdir fallback under-performs, H-A requires additional mitigation (app-level retry on conflict) OR architectural revisiting.

prototyperB.deliverable: Minimal plugin hooks/hooks.json exercise implementing 3 validation phases: (a) SessionStart+PostToolUse(schemas/ontology/*.ts) Design+Compile phase runs tsc --noEmit, (b) mock MCP commit_edits pre-flight Runtime phase evaluates a toy submission criterion, (c) PostToolUse(Edit) Post-Write phase runs a drift probe. Emits validation_phase_completed events to a fixture events.jsonl.
evalSuiteTargets: Unit: Design+Compile phase catches deliberate TS type error, emits validation_phase_completed(phase=compile, passed=false, errorClass=TypeError)
Unit: Runtime phase catches submission criterion violation, emits validation_phase_completed(phase=runtime, passed=false, errorClass=SubmissionCriteriaFailed)
Unit: Post-Write phase catches simulated drift, emits validation_phase_completed(phase=post_write, passed=false, errorClass=DriftDetected)
Integration: run hooks against fixture palantir-math subset; measure per-hook latency
Adversarial: 50 sequential Edits in a loop — verify hook latency stays under threshold (document measured latency)
prototyperB.buildSuccessCriteria: `bun test` passes all 5 eval cases; hook handlers run under 500ms per invocation on the fixture.
prototyperB.failureMode: If hook latency exceeds tolerable threshold OR a 4th distinct error class appears that doesn't fit 3 phases, H-C is invalidated and full 6-phase becomes candidate.
prototyperB.note: Prototyper-b can run in parallel with prototyper-a; no shared state between the two scopes. Coordination via events.jsonl fixture happens at read-time only.

nextSpawnRecommendation.instruction: Spawn prototyper-a for T7 and prototyper-b for T8 in parallel. Each operates in its own worktree. Both write to ontology-state/eval-results.json (their file ownership per registry). Both report back to eval-runner (T9) who generates and runs the formal eval suites.
preconditions: T5 and T6 TaskUpdate to completed
SendMessage to team lead with this summary

prototyper-b emits PrototypeResult for H-C with buildStatus + buildLog
